description:
A CFD analyst is preparing the eCRM-001 transport model for a transonic drag simulation. To resolve the complex flow gradients around the empennage without exceeding memory limits, you must configure a multi-resolution surface mesh using OpenVSP's CFD Mesh tool. 

1. Open the file at /home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/eCRM-001_wing_tail.vsp3 in OpenVSP.
2. Read the refinement specification file located at /home/ga/Desktop/mesh_refinement_spec.txt.
3. Open the CFD Mesh tool (Analysis > CFD Mesh).
4. Set the Global Max Edge Length to 0.40 m.
5. Navigate to the Sources tab and add a new refinement source of type 'Box'.
6. Configure the Box source parameters according to the specification (Center X/Y/Z, Extents Length/Width/Height, and Target Edge Length).
7. Execute the CFD mesh generation.
8. Export the resulting mesh in Cart3D (.tri) format to /home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/exports/eCRM001_refined.tri.
9. Save the modified OpenVSP model to /home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/eCRM001_mesh_setup.vsp3 so the CFD team can review your mesh settings.
